agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Parcel delivery logistics platform managing shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers. Core workflow: create shipment with addresses, transition through states, charge based on weight bracket.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management — covers shipment creation, state lifecycle, weight validation, and pricing classification.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A domain entity representing a parcel moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, with a defined weight and lifecycle state."},{"term":"Pickup Address","definition":"The origin location where the parcel is collected. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Delivery Address","definition":"The destination location where the parcel is delivered. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Shipment State","definition":"The current lifecycle phase of a shipment. Valid states: Pending, In Transit, Delivered."},{"term":"Pending","definition":"Initial state of a newly created shipment, before it begins moving."},{"term":"In Transit","definition":"State indicating the shipment is actively being transported. Irreversible — cannot return to Pending."},{"term":"Delivered","definition":"Terminal state indicating the parcel has reached its delivery address. No further state transitions allowed."},{"term":"Weight","definition":"The mass of the parcel in kilograms. Must be positive and at most 1000 kg."},{"term":"Weight Bracket","definition":"A pricing classification derived from shipment weight: Standard (0–10 kg], Heavy (10–50 kg], Freight (>50 kg)."},{"term":"Standard","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 0 kg up to and including 10 kg."},{"term":"Heavy","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 10 kg up to and including 50 kg."},{"term":"Freight","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 50 kg up to and including 1000 kg."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment without both endpoints is physically meaningless — there is no parcel movement without an origin and destination. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create a shipment with a missing or empty pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A newly created shipment enters the Pending state.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"State integrity: every shipment must have a known initial state. A shipment that exists without a state corrupts the system.","testable_assertion":"After successful creation, shipment.state == Pending."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A shipment in the In Transit state cannot transition back to Pending.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — a parcel that has left the origin cannot un-leave. Allowing reversal would corrupt tracking, billing, and driver dispatch state.","testable_assertion":"Calling transition-to-Pending on a shipment with state In Transit must be rejected and leave state unchanged."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A shipment in the Delivered state cannot transition to any other state. Delivered is a terminal state.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Finality constraint — delivery is a real-world event that has occurred. Reversing it would break chain-of-custody, invoicing, and proof-of-delivery records.","testable_assertion":"Any state transition attempted on a shipment with state Delivered must be rejected and leave state unchanged."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Shipment weight must be strictly greater than zero.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A parcel with zero or negative weight is physically impossible and would break pricing calculations.","testable_assertion":"Creating or updating a shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational policy cap, not a physical law. The business could raise this limit for oversized freight partnerships. A VP decision could change this threshold.","testable_assertion":"Creating or updating a shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Weight bracket is Standard when weight is in the range (0, 10] kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing tier boundaries are business policy. Marketing or finance could adjust bracket thresholds.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight 10 kg must be classified as Standard; a shipment with weight 0.5 kg must be classified as Standard."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Weight bracket is Heavy when weight is in the range (10, 50] kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing tier boundary — business policy, adjustable.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight 10.1 kg must be classified as Heavy; a shipment with weight 50 kg must be classified as Heavy."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"Weight bracket is Freight when weight is in the range (50, 1000] kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing tier boundary — business policy, adjustable.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight 50.1 kg must be classified as Freight; a shipment with weight 1000 kg must be classified as Freight."},{"id":"BR-10","rule":"The only valid state transitions are: Pending → In Transit, and In Transit → Delivered.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"State machine integrity. Any transition not in this set (e.g., Pending → Delivered, Delivered → anything, In Transit → Pending) would violate the physical shipment lifecycle and corrupt downstream systems.","testable_assertion":"Only the transitions Pending→InTransit and InTransit→Delivered succeed; all other transitions must be rejected."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A valid pickup address, delivery address, and weight of 5 kg","when":"A shipment is created","then":"The shipment is persisted with state Pending, the provided addresses, and weight bracket Standard."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A creation request missing the pickup address","when":"A shipment creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error; no shipment is created."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A creation request missing the delivery address","when":"A shipment creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error; no shipment is created."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"The shipment is transitioned to In Transit","then":"The shipment state becomes In Transit."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"A transition to Pending is attempted","then":"The transition is rejected; the shipment remains In Transit."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"The shipment is transitioned to Delivered","then":"The shipment state becomes Delivered."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A shipment in Delivered state","when":"Any state transition is attempted (to Pending or In Transit)","then":"The transition is rejected; the shipment remains Delivered."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"A transition directly to Delivered is attempted","then":"The transition is rejected; the shipment remains Pending."},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A creation request with weight 0 kg","when":"A shipment creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error."},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A creation request with weight -5 kg","when":"A shipment creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error."},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A creation request with weight 1001 kg","when":"A shipment creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error."},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A creation request with weight 10 kg","when":"A shipment is created","then":"The weight bracket is Standard."},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A creation request with weight 10.01 kg","when":"A shipment is created","then":"The weight bracket is Heavy."},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A creation request with weight 50 kg","when":"A shipment is created","then":"The weight bracket is Heavy."},{"id":"AC-15","given":"A creation request with weight 50.01 kg","when":"A shipment is created","then":"The weight bracket is Freight."},{"id":"AC-16","given":"A creation request with weight 1000 kg","when":"A shipment is created","then":"The weight bracket is Freight and the shipment is accepted (at the maximum)."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Weight exactly at bracket boundary: 10 kg","expected_behavior":"Classified as Standard (upper-inclusive). The spec says '0-10 kg is standard' implying 10 kg is included in Standard."},{"scenario":"Weight exactly at bracket boundary: 50 kg","expected_behavior":"Classified as Heavy. The spec says '10-50 kg is heavy' implying 50 kg is included in Heavy."},{"scenario":"Weight exactly at 1000 kg","expected_behavior":"Accepted and classified as Freight. 1000 kg is the maximum, not above it."},{"scenario":"Weight with high decimal precision (e.g., 10.0000001 kg)","expected_behavior":"Classified as Heavy. Bracket boundaries must handle floating-point precision correctly."},{"scenario":"Attempting Pending → Delivered (skipping In Transit)","expected_behavior":"Rejected. The spec implies a linear state progression: Pending → In Transit → Delivered. Direct skip violates the lifecycle."},{"scenario":"Re-delivering an already Delivered shipment","expected_behavior":"Rejected. Delivered is terminal; no re-delivery or duplicate delivery transitions."},{"scenario":"Pickup address and delivery address are identical","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — spec does not address this. Logistically suspicious but not explicitly forbidden. Recommend clarifying with product."},{"scenario":"Extremely small weight (e.g., 0.001 kg)","expected_behavior":"Accepted as Standard bracket. Weight is positive and under 10 kg."},{"scenario":"Updating weight after shipment is In Transit or Delivered","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — spec does not address mutability of weight after creation. Needs clarification. Changing weight post-transit would affect billing integrity."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Weight bracket misclassification directly impacts revenue — undercharging Heavy as Standard or Freight as Heavy loses margin. Correct bracket enforcement is a billing integrity concern.","operational":"State machine enforcement prevents invalid dispatch operations (e.g., re-dispatching a delivered parcel). The 1000 kg cap protects fleet capacity and prevents bookings that exceed vehicle payload limits.","user":"Clear state transitions provide customers reliable tracking. Rejected invalid transitions prevent confusing status changes. Correct weight classification sets delivery expectations (standard vs freight handling).","risk":"Primary risk is bracket boundary ambiguity — the spec uses informal ranges ('0-10 kg', '10-50 kg') without specifying inclusive/exclusive bounds. An off-by-one at boundaries affects pricing for every shipment at those weights. Secondary risk: spec is silent on weight mutability post-creation, which could be exploited to change billing tier after dispatch.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (Shippers)","impact":"Pricing transparency — must see correct bracket at booking. Invalid state transitions being rejected prevents confusion in shipment tracking."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Weight bracket accuracy directly determines invoice amounts. Boundary errors cause systematic under- or over-charging."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"State machine integrity ensures drivers are not dispatched to already-delivered shipments. Weight cap ensures fleet compatibility."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Terminal Delivered state prevents erroneous re-opens. Clear state rules reduce ambiguous status inquiries."},{"group":"Warehouse / Fulfillment","impact":"Weight validation at creation prevents impossible-to-handle parcels from entering the system."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is clear on state transitions and weight constraints but uses informal range notation ('0-10', '10-50', 'over 50') that creates ambiguity at exact boundaries. The spec is also silent on several important operational questions: weight mutability after creation, whether addresses can be identical, whether addresses can be changed post-creation, and what triggers state transitions. No contradictions found, but gaps exist.","high_confidence_areas":["State machine lifecycle: Pending → In Transit → Delivered with no backward transitions","Delivered is terminal","Weight must be positive and <= 1000 kg","Three weight brackets exist: Standard, Heavy, Freight","Both addresses required at creation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact boundary inclusivity for weight brackets (is 10 kg Standard or Heavy?)","Whether weight or addresses can be mutated after shipment creation","Whether Pending → Delivered skip is allowed or only sequential transitions","Same pickup and delivery address validity","Whether the 1000 kg cap is a hard physical limit or adjustable policy"],"assumptions":["Weight bracket boundaries are upper-inclusive: Standard includes 10 kg, Heavy includes 50 kg, Freight includes 1000 kg","State transitions are strictly sequential: Pending → In Transit → Delivered only; no skipping","Shipment creation always starts in Pending state — there is no way to create a shipment directly in another state","Weight is set at creation time; spec does not mention post-creation weight changes"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Weight bracket boundary inclusivity is not specified. The spec says '0-10 kg is standard, 10-50 kg is heavy, over 50 kg is freight' — the value 10 kg belongs to both the first and second descriptions, and 50 kg could be Heavy or Freight depending on interpretation.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance / Billing","Customers (Shippers)"],"options":["A: Upper-inclusive (Standard ≤10, Heavy ≤50, Freight >50) — natural reading of 'over 50'","B: Lower-inclusive (Standard <10, Heavy <50, Freight ≥50)","C: Explicit clarification from product on each boundary"],"recommendation":"Option A (upper-inclusive for lower bracket) — 'over 50 kg is freight' most naturally means >50, making 50 kg Heavy and 10 kg Standard. But this should be confirmed with product."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Spec does not address whether shipment weight or addresses can be modified after creation. Weight mutability has direct billing implications — if weight can change after In Transit, the pricing bracket could shift post-dispatch.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance / Billing","Operations / Dispatch"],"options":["A: Weight and addresses are immutable after creation","B: Weight and addresses are mutable only while in Pending state","C: Weight and addresses are always mutable (risky for billing integrity)"],"recommendation":"Option B — allow changes only while Pending. Once In Transit, the physical parcel exists and its weight is fixed. This protects billing integrity."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Spec does not explicitly state whether Pending → Delivered is a valid transition (skipping In Transit). The spec only says 'once in transit, it cannot be sent back to pending' and 'delivered shipments are final', but does not enumerate the full valid transition set.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations / Dispatch","Customer Support"],"options":["A: Only sequential transitions allowed (Pending→InTransit, InTransit→Delivered)","B: Allow skip (Pending→Delivered) for edge cases like in-store pickup"],"recommendation":"Option A — strict sequential transitions. The spec describes a standard logistics flow, and skipping In Transit would bypass tracking and dispatch workflows. If skip is needed, it should be a deliberate product decision."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
